task_completion.description: The Python script initializes the e-puck's proximity sensors and motors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code visible in the editor containing strings like 'ps0' to 'ps7', 'left wheel motor', and 'right wheel motor' being retrieved using the Webots API (e.g., robot.getDevice(...)).

task_completion.description: The script contains a main Webots control loop.

task_completion.description: The script implements logic to read sensor values and adjust motor speeds to avoid obstacles.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor or IDE to write the code.

integrity.description: The code uses the genuine Webots Python API.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script includes 'from controller import Robot' and uses standard Webots methods rather than a mocked or hardcoded print script.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with Webots to save the world or properly copied the .wbt file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The controller script must be located exactly at /home/ga/webots_projects/epuck_obstacle/controllers/obstacle_avoider/obstacle_avoider.py. The script must control an 'e-puck' robot, specifically utilizing proximity sensors named 'ps0' through 'ps7' and motors named 'left wheel motor' and 'right wheel motor'. The final Webots world file must be saved exactly to /home/ga/Desktop/epuck_avoidance.wbt.
